steps.title: Identify the relationship between the examples
steps.content: Both Elizabeth Alexander's and Cornelius Eady's examples show poets exploring paintings—they're parallel illustrations of ekphrastic poetry. The signal is that the second example is similar to the first, not contrasting or causally connected to it.

steps.title: Choose the word that expresses this relationship
steps.content:
The second example needs a word showing it's similar to the first. “Likewise” means “in the same way,” which is precisely what's needed—Eady's poem exemplifies the same ekphrastic practice as Alexander's.

The others signal contrast (nevertheless, conversely) or causation (hence), neither of which applies to parallel examples.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
